memoryArtifacts.headline: Elizabeth Forrester
memoryArtifacts.description: Elizabeth Forester was the daughter of James Forester of Corstorphine. A contract for the marriage of Elizabeth Forester and David Makgill was signed on 11 May 1557. She died on 16 March 1578/79.

popularStory.headline: Elizabeth Forrester
popularStory.description: Elizabeth Forester was the daughter of James Forester of Corstorphine. A contract for the marriage of Elizabeth Forester and David Makgill was signed on 11 May 1557. She died on 16 March 1578/79.

zh.lifeSummary: 當Elizabeth Forrester於大約1519年出生在蘇格蘭艾斯爾艾爾時，她的父親Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden26歲，母親Elizabeth Cunningham24歲。她於1557年5月11日與David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire在蘇格蘭結婚。他們至少是8個兒子和2個女兒的父母。她去世於1578年3月16日在蘇格蘭米德洛迪安可藍斯頓享年60歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Elizabeth Forrester于大约1519年出生在苏格兰艾斯爾艾爾时，她的父亲Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden26岁，母亲Elizabeth Cunningham24岁。她于1557年5月11日与David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire在苏格兰结婚。他们至少是8个儿子和2个女儿的父母。她去世于1578年3月16日在苏格兰米德洛迪安可藍斯頓享年60岁。

nameMeanings.name: Forrester
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name from Middle English forester ‘officer in charge of a forest or lord's hunting ground’ (Old French forestier, Anglo-Norman forester). See Forrest .

variants.name: Forrest
meanings: English: topographic name for someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or a metonymic occupational name for a keeper or worker in one. Middle English forest was not, as today, a near-synonym of wood, but referred specifically to a large area of woodland reserved by law for the purposes of hunting by the king and his nobles. The same applied to the European cognates, both ancient Germanic and Romance. The English word is from Middle English forest ‘forest’, Old French forest, Late Latin forestis (silva). This is generally taken to be a derivative of foris ‘outside’; the reference was probably to woods lying outside a settlement. On the other hand, Middle High German for(e)st has been held to be a derivative of Old High German foraha ‘fir’ (see Forster ), with the addition of a collective suffix.
Irish: this name is also frequently attested in Ireland, where it may be a variant of   Forrestal  .

variants.name: Forester
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Forrester   (see   Forrest  ).

variants.name: Forrister
meanings: English and Scottish: variant of   Forrester  .

variants.name: Forster
meanings: English: shortened form of   Forrester  , an occupational name for someone who worked in a forest.
English: possibly a Norman French nickname or occupational name from Old French forcetier ‘maker of scissors or shears, cutler’. Compare Force and Forse .

German (mainly Förster): agent derivative of Middle High German forst ‘forest’, denoting someone who lived in or near a royal forest, or who worked in one (see Forst ). This surname is also found in France (Alsace and Lorraine). Compare Foerster .

nameMeanings.name: Elizabeth
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: The usual spelling of   Elisabeth   in English. It is recorded in the medieval period, but was made popular by being borne by  Queen Elizabeth I  of England (  1533–1603 ). In the 20th century it again became extremely fashionable, partly because it was the name of  Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon  (  1900–2002 ), who in  1936  became Queen Elizabeth as the wife of King George VI, and after his death in  1952  achieved great public affection as Queen Mother for nearly half a century. Even more influentially, it is the name of her daughter  Queen Elizabeth II  (  b. 1926 ).

variants.name: Elisabeth
meanings: The spelling of Elizabeth used in the Authorized Version of the New Testament, and in most modern European languages. This was the name of the mother of John the Baptist (Luke 1:60). Etymologically, the name means ‘God is my oath’, and is therefore identical with Elisheba, the name of the wife of Aaron according to the genealogy at Exodus 6:23. The final element seems to have been altered by association with Hebrew shabbāth ‘Sabbath’.

en.lifeSummary: When Elizabeth Forrester was born about 1519, in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, her father, Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden, was 26 and her mother, Elizabeth Cunningham, was 24. She married David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire on 11 May 1557, in Scotland. They were the parents of at least 8 sons and 2 daughters. She died on 16 March 1578, in Cranston, Midlothian, Scotland, at the age of 60.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Elizabeth Forrester est née environ 1519, à Ayr, Ayrshire, Écosse, son père, Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden, avait 26 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Cunningham, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire le 11 mai 1557, à Écosse. Ils ont eu au moins 8 fils et 2 filles. Elle est décédée le 16 mars 1578, à Cranston, Midlothian, Écosse, à l’âge de 60 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Elizabeth Forrester um 1519 in Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden 26 und ihre Mutter Elizabeth Cunningham 24. Sie hat am 11. Mai 1557 in Scotland David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 8 Söhne und 2 Töchter. Sie starb am 16. März 1578 im Alter von 60 Jahren in Cranston, Midlothian, Scotland.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Forrester è nata il circa 1519 nel Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, sua padre, Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden, aveva 26 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Cunningham, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire il 11 maggio 1557, nel Scotland. Erano genitori di almeno 8 figli e 2 figlie. Morì il 16 marzo 1578, nel Cranston, Midlothian, Scotland, all'età di 60 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Forresterが約1519年にスコットランドエイルシュアイアエイルで生まれた時，父Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Gardenは26歳，母Elizabeth Cunninghamは24歳であった。1557年5月11日にスコットランドでDavid MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshireと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも8人の息子と2人の娘の親であった。彼女は1578年3月16日にスコットランドミドロスアンクランストンで60歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Elizabeth Forrester(이)가 대략 1519년 스컷랜드 에이르실르 에이르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden(은)는 26세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Cunningham(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1557년 5월 11일 스컷랜드에서 David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여덟 명의 아들과 두 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1578년 3월 16일 스컷랜드 미들로스이언 크랜스턴에서 60세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Elizabeth Forrester nasceu aproximadamente 1519, em Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, seu pai, Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden, tinha 26 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Cunningham, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire em 11 de maio de 1557, em Scotland. Eles tiveram pelo menos 8 filhos e 2 filhas. Ela faleceu em 16 de março de 1578, em Cranston, Midlothian, Scotland, com 60 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Elizabeth Forrester родилась около 1519 г. в местности аир, аиршир, Шотландия, её отцу по имени Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden было 26 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Cunningham, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire 11 мая 1557 г. в местности Шотландия. У них было по крайней мере 8 сыновей и 2 дочери. Она умерла 16 марта 1578 г. в местности кранстон, мидлотян, Шотландия в возрасте 60 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Elizabeth Forrester nació aproximadamente 1519, en Ayr, Ayrshire, Scotland, su papá, Sir James Forrester 8th of torwood and 3rd of Garden, tenía 26 años y su mamá, Elizabeth Cunningham, tenía 24 años. Se casó con David MacGill of Kirkcudbrightshire el 11 de mayo de 1557, en Scotland. Fueron padres de por lo menos 8 hijos y 2 hijas. Murió el 16 de marzo de 1578, en Cranston, Midlothian, Scotland, a la edad de 60 años.
